original: Erachus becomes Bishop of Liège
160 Jews are burned at the stake in Chinon, France
Portuguese expedition under King John I attacks the Moroccan city of Ceuta and takes it a day later, beginning Portuguese overseas expansion
Spain begins the siege of Alkmaar in the Netherlands
Deed of Transfer proclaims the Netherlands' independence
Sea battle at Kijkduin: De Ruyter defeats English and French fleets
Pueblo Indians take possession of Santa Fe in the New Mexican Province from the Spanish
Battle of Dunkeld is fought in Scotland between supporters of King James VII of Scotland and troops of William of Orange
The church (later cathedral) of "Our Lady of Candlemas of Mayagüez" is founded in Mayagüez, Puerto Rico, establishing the basis for the city's foundation
A jury in Sheffield, Massachusetts, grants two slaves, Bett (later Elizabeth Freeman) and Bron, their freedom after Freeman brings the case to court
Jean-Baptiste Bernadotte, Marshal of France, is elected Crown Prince of Sweden by the Swedish Riksdag of the Estates
Jarvis Island is discovered by the crew of the ship Eliza Frances
John Hampton patents the Venetian blind
Hobart, Tasmania, Australia, is declared a city
Tlingit Indians destroy Fort Selkirk, Yukon Territory
America's first consul to Japan, Townsend Harris, arrives in Shimoda (traditional Japanese date: July 21, 1856)
First Lincoln-Douglas debate in Illinois
The Vienna Stadtpark, the first public park and garden in Vienna, Austria, opens its gates
Battle at Globe Tavern, Virginia, ends with 2,500 casualties
Battle of Grubbs Crossroads, Kentucky
Battle of Summit Point, Virginia
Major General Nathan B. Forrest assaults Memphis, Tennessee
American Bar Association organizes in Saratoga, New York
Surrey wicket-keeper Ted Pooley completes a first-class cricket record of eight stumpings in a county match against Kent at The Oval
The Virgin Mary, along with St. Joseph and St. John the Evangelist, reportedly appears to the people of Knock, County Mayo, Ireland
Dutch Mackay government resigns
Oldsmobile is first manufactured by Olds Motor Vehicle Co. in Lansing, Michigan

Baltimore Orioles pitcher Joe McGinnity is suspended from MLB for punching and spitting on umpire Tom Connolly in the previous day's 5-2 loss to the Detroit Tigers; the lifetime suspension is reduced to 12 days
The Mona Lisa is stolen from the Louvre Museum by Italian decorator Vincenzo Peruggia, recovered in 1913
Francis Carter-Cotton is chosen as the first chancellor of the University of British Columbia
French offensive along the Sambre River in the Ardennes, Belgium
German troops occupy Tamines, Belgium
Italy declares war on Turkey in World War I
Sir Roger Casement, an Ulster Protestant and ardent Irish nationalist, arrested by the British
Dutch football club FC Emmen is formed in the town of Emmen in the northeastern province of Drenthe in the Netherlands
Chicago White Sox pitcher Ted Lyons no-hits the Boston Red Sox 6-0 in just 67 minutes at Fenway Park
Uprising against Greek President and dictator Pangalos leads to the arrest of Pangalos and the return to power by Kountouriotis
Fourth Pan-African Congress meets in New York City
Chicago Cardinals become the first professional football team to train out of town
Prohibition of Wieringermeer finishes
Italy bars all Jewish teachers in public and high schools

Attorney Samuel Tucker organizes a peaceful sit-in at the public library in Alexandria, Virginia, in an effort to grant Black patrons equal access; five young men are arrested for disorderly conduct. No trial is ever held, and charges are officially dropped 80 years later, and the community builds a separate facility nearby
Transport #22 departs with French Jews to Nazi-Germany
World War II: German soldiers plant the Nazi flag on Mount Elbrus in the Caucasus
Japan leaves Aleutian Islands
USSR names Andrei Gromyko as its ambassador to the United States
Battle of Falaise, Normandy, led by Polish Armoured Division and First Canadian Army, is the largest encirclement on the Western Front, leading to the surrender and capture of the German 7th Army
Germans storm up Hill 262 (Mont Ormel) in Normandy
Grieg, Wright, and Forrest's musical "Song of Norway" premieres in NYC
Raid on Jewish children's house in Secrétan and St-Mandé
US 12th Army Corps occupies Sens
US President Harry Truman ends Lend-Lease program
Cleveland Indians 47-inning scoreless streak is broken as future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcher Bob Lemon yields a home run to Aaron Robinson in a 3-2 loss to Chicago White Sox
Canada's largest earthquake since 1700 strikes the Queen Charlotte Fault below Haida Gwaii at 8.1 magnitude, knocking cows off their feet
Philadelphia fans cause the A's to forfeit the game when they riot over a trapped line drive by Richie Ashburn, with the Giants leading 4-2 in the 9th inning, declared winners
Marion Carl in Douglas Skyrocket reaches a record of 25,370 meters
Sultan Sidi Mohammed Am Joessoef V of Morocco is deposed
WTVW TV channel 7 in Evansville, IN (ABC) begins broadcasting
First launch of the R-7 "Semyorka" rocket, an intercontinental ballistic missile, in Baikonur, Kazakhstan
The Soviet Union successfully tests the world's first intercontinental ballistic missile, the R-7, which flies over 6,000 km (3,700 mi)
KUT-FM in Austin, Texas, begins radio transmissions
Hawaii becomes the 50th state of the United States
Verne Gagne defeats Mister M (Doctor X) in Minneapolis to become NWA champion
Jerry Lynch's record 15th pinch-hit home run gives Pirates a 7-6 victory
Martial law is declared in South Vietnam following raids on Buddhist pagodas
Gemini V launched into orbit, carrying astronauts Gordon Cooper and Charles "Pete" Conrad
Romania adopts a constitution
The Crusher beats Mad Dog Vachon in St. Paul to become NWA champion

The Beatles headline their second show of the day at Busch Memorial Stadium in St. Louis, Missouri, after playing in the afternoon 341 miles away in Cincinnati, Ohio, following a rainout the night before
China reports the downing of two US bombers
Liquid gas tanker explodes in Martelange, Belgium, killing 22
Mikis Theodorakis is arrested in Greece

MLB Kansas City Athletics utility player Ken Harrelson becomes baseball's first free agent when he is abruptly released by the team after he reportedly refers to team owner Charlie Finley as "a menace to baseball"
After five years, the Soviet Union once again jams Voice of America radio
PFC James Anderson Jr. is the first African American Marine to receive the Medal of Honor in the Vietnam War
Fire in Al-Aqsa-mosque in Jerusalem
First Gap clothing store opens on Ocean Avenue, San Francisco
British dock strike ends after dockers accept an amended Jones-Adlington Agreement
First hot air balloon flight over the Alps
Grace Slick is maced by police when a band official calls cops pigs
Republican Convention opens in Miami Beach
US orbiting astronomy observatory Copernicus is launched
Rick and Paul Reuschel become the first brothers to pitch a combined shutout
The US eases the trade embargo against Cuba
Three-truck pile-up kills 10 and injures 26 on French highway
Al Bumbry hits 17th inside-the-park home run in Oriole history
Battle, East Sussex: Mary Langdon becomes the first British firewoman

Operation Paul Bunyan begins in retaliation for the Korean axe murder incident three days prior, deploying 110 troops, 27 helicopters, and three B-52 bombers to the Korean Demilitarized Zone to cut down a poplar blocking the view of UN observers
American water skier Donna Patterson Brice sets women's high speed record 111.11 mph at Long Beach, California
Release of first gay-themed telefilm, "Matlovich vs. U.S. Air Force," based on the true story of Leonard Matlovich, a US Air Force sergeant who is discharged from the Air Force for being gay
Mets win a protested game against Astros 5-0
Russian Bolshoi Ballet dancer Alexander Godunov defects in New York City, joins the American Ballet Theatre, and works as a film actor
British drummer and band co-founder Bill Ward quits Black Sabbath for the first time
People for the Ethical Treatment of Animals (PETA) is founded in a basement office in Takoma Park, Maryland
Milwaukee Brewers relief pitcher Rollie Fingers becomes the first pitcher to achieve 300 saves
Palestinian terrorists are dispersed from Beirut
110°F (43°C) recorded in Fayetteville, North Carolina (state record)
American Mary Decker Slaney runs a mile in a world record time of 4:16.71 in Zurich, Switzerland; it is the third time she holds the record
NY Lotto pays $41 million to three winners (numbers are 14-17-22-23-30-47)
Lake Nyos volcanic eruption in Cameroon releases a poisonous cloud of carbon dioxide, killing 1,746 people and 3,500 livestock
Red Sox Spike Owen scores 6 runs in a 24-5 rout of the Cleveland Indians
Suriname's Ronnie Brunswijk's Jungle Commandos kill two government officials
With two outs in the sixth inning, the Red Sox score 11 runs
Clayton Lonetree, the first US Marine court-martialed for spying, is convicted
Mack Lobell sets harness racing's trotting mile record (1:52)
Silke Hörner swims the women's world record in the 100m breaststroke (1:07.91)
Latvia declares its independence from USSR
US Marshals move in on Randy Weaver's cabin in Ruby Ridge, Idaho, to apprehend him on firearms charges; an 11-day standoff ensues
NASA loses contact with Mars Observer
Ernesto Zedillo wins the Mexican presidential election
Royal Air Maroc ATR-42 crashes at Agadir, killing 44
The last French troops pull out of Rwanda, ending their highly controversial mission
Typhoon Fred ravages Chinese county Zhejiang, killing 700+
Atlantic Southeast Airlines Flight 529 crashes near Carrollton, Georgia, killing 9 crew members and passengers
In Venezuela, a subsidiary of state-owned Petroleos de Venezuela (PdVSA), Corpoven, signs a memorandum of understanding (MOU) with U.S.-based ARCO
Netscape Browser 3.0 is released
Typhoon Winnie kills 140 and injures 3,000 in East China
US government forces closure of Hudson Foods due to E. coli outbreak
"How You Remind Me" single released by Nickelback (Billboard Song of the Year 2002)
NATO decides to send a peacekeeping force to the former Yugoslav Republic of Macedonia
The Red Cross announces that a famine is striking Tajikistan and calls for international financial aid for Tajikistan and Uzbekistan
Belarusian sprinter Yulia Nestsiarenka runs 10.93 to beat American Lauryn Williams and win the 100 m gold medal at the Athens Olympics

Nicolás Massú and Fernando González win the men's doubles tennis at the Athens Olympics, defeating the German pair Nicolas Kiefer and Rainer Schüttler in 5 sets; it is Chile's first Olympic gold medal

South Africa clinches its second Tri Nations Rugby Series on points differential with a 23-19 win over Australia in Durban; the Wallabies score 3 tries to 2, but Percy Montgomery lands 5 goals for the Springboks
The archery competition concludes at the Athens Olympics with South Korea dominating by winning 3 out of 4 gold medals; the venue is the Panathenaic Stadium, where the 1896 games were held
The Australian women's 4 x 100 m medley relay team of Giaan Rooney, Leisel Jones, Petria Thomas, and Jodie Henry smash the world record to take gold in 3:57.32 at the Athens Olympics
Hurricane Dean, the first storm since Hurricane Andrew to make landfall as a Category 5, makes landfall in Costa Maya, Mexico, with winds at 165 mph
After winning the event in Athens, Jamaican sprinter Veronica Campbell-Brown wins consecutive 200 m Olympic titles, beating American Allyson Felix by 0.19 seconds in Beijing

American pair Misty May-Treanor and Kerri Walsh dominate China's Tian Jia and Wang Jie 21-18, 21-18 in the beach volleyball final to repeat their gold medal from Athens; they don't lose a set in the competition
An American sweep of the 400 m medals at the Beijing Olympics; LaShawn Merritt wins gold in 43.75 ahead of Jeremy Wariner and David Neville
Daniëlle de Bruijn scores a match-winning 7 goals as the Netherlands upsets the United States 9-8 to take the women's water polo gold medal at the Beijing Olympics
Dutch swimmer and cancer survivor Maarten van der Weijden wins inaugural men's open water marathon (10 km) in 1:51:51.6 at the Beijing Olympics
Japan edges the United States 3-1 for the gold medal in the final of the women's softball tournament at the Beijing Olympics

New Zealand clinches their 10th Tri Nations Rugby Series with one match remaining, scoring 2 tries in the last 3 minutes to beat South Africa 29–22 in the first-ever Test at FNB Stadium in Johannesburg
Twenty people die from the Ebola virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo
21 people are killed in flash floods in Qinghai province, China
37 people are killed and 16 are injured in a bus crash near Chin Swee Temple, Malaysia
Israeli airstrike in Rafah kills Mohammed Abu Shammala, Raed al-Atar, and Mohammed Barhoum, three of Hamas's top commanders
European Refugee Crisis: Germany makes it easier for Syrian refugees to claim asylum by suspending their Dublin Regulations
First British unmanned drone hits a UK citizen outside a conflict: ISIS fighter Reyaad Khan in Raqqa, Syria
Terrorist attack on train between Amsterdam and Paris is thwarted by four passengers overpowering the gunman
Brazil wins its third Olympic men's indoor volleyball gold medal at the Rio Games with a 25–22, 28–26, 26–24 victory over Italy
XXXI Summer Olympic Games close at the Estadio Maracanã, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
Chile's Constitutional Court approves a bill to ease the country's total abortion ban
Destroyer USS John S. McCain collides with an oil tanker near Singapore, leaving 10 missing and 5 injured
Johnson & Johnson is ordered to pay $417 million to a woman who develops ovarian cancer after using their talc-based products
London's Parliament clock, Big Ben, chimes for the last time before a four-year restoration process for its tower begins
Total solar eclipse visible from North America
California Representative Duncan Hunter is indicted for using campaign funds for personal expenses, including holidays and a flight for a pet rabbit
Paul Manafort, former Trump campaign chairman, is convicted on eight counts of fraud in federal court in Alexandria, Virginia
Water ice is first detected on the Moon by India's Chandrayaan-1 spacecraft (2008-9) in findings published by scientists
74,155 fires caused by land clearing are burning in the Amazon rainforest, the most ever recorded, according to Brazil's National Institute for Space Research
German Chancellor challenges British PMBoris Johnsonto find a solution to a no-deal Brexit in 30 days during their meeting in Berlin
US PresidentDonald Trumpsays Danish PM Mette Frederiksen is "nasty" to him over his interest in buying Greenland and cancels his trip to Denmark
American actress Lori Loughlin is sentenced to two months in prison along with her husband for her role in the US college admissions bribery scandal
Flash flooding in Humphreys County, Middle Tennessee, kills 22, with dozens missing
Masai Graham wins funniest joke at Edinburgh Fringe with "I tried to steal spaghetti from the shop, but the female guard saw me and I couldn't get pasta."
Charles Martinet, the voice of Mario in Nintendo's video games, retires after 27 years
Saudi border guards are accused of killing hundreds of migrants, shooting them as they try to cross into the country from Yemen in a report by Human Rights Watch
Afghanistan's Taliban regime issues new vice and virtue laws banning the sound of women's voices or singing in public and traveling anywhere without a male escort
A 1642 will at the center of a legal battle over Shakespeare's home is found after 150 years in an unlabeled box at the National Archives
Egyptian authorities unveil parts of a 2,000-year-old sunken city submerged off the coast of Alexandria, including buildings, statues, artifacts, and an ancient dock
Kublai Khanaccepts the surrender of his younger brother Ariq Böke at Xanadu, at the end of the Mongol civil war
Ottoman Turks underSuleiman the Magnificentcapture Buda, the capital of the Hungarian Kingdom and go on to dominate central Hungary for 158 years until the 1699 Treaty of Karlowitz
Future Danish astronomer 14-year-oldTycho Brahebecomes interested in astronomy after observing a solar eclipse
KingLouis XIVdonates 600 books to found the library of the Académie Française
Edirne Incident: Turkish army ousts SultanMustafa II, replacing him with his brother Ahmed III, reducing the power of the Sultan
KingGustav IIIof Sweden completes a coup d'état by adopting a new constitution and installing himself as an enlightened despot, ending fifty years of parliamentary rule

Battle of Vimeiro: British and Portuguese forces led by GeneralArthur Wellesleydefeat French force under Major-General Jean-Andoche Junot near the village of Vimeiro, Portugal, the first Anglo-Portuguese victory of the Peninsular War
Former slaveNat Turnerleads uprising against slavery

Lawrence Massacre [Quantrill's raid]: 450 Confederate guerrillas led by William Quantrill, withWilliam T. AndersonandFrank James, attack the Unionist town of Lawrence, Kansas, killing more than 150 unarmed men and boys
Henry Morton Stanleymakes his maiden speech in the UK House of Commons as the Member for North Lambeth in a discussion on Africa
Yankees sluggerBabe Ruthbecomes the first MLB player to hit 600 career home runs as NY defeats the St. Louis Browns 11-7 at Sportsman's Park
Cleveland Indians pitcherWes Ferrellis the first to win 20 games in each of his first four seasons
Walt Disney's animated movie "Bambi," based on the book byFelix Salten, is released
Baseball player representativesRalph Kiner(NL) andAllie Reynolds(AL) hire labor leader John Norman Lewis for $15,000 to give legal advice to players in negotiations with team owners
Chicago teenagerEmmett Tillarrives in Money, Mississippi, to visit relatives, a week before he is murdered
American country singerPatsy Clinerecords struggling songwriter Willie Nelson's song "Crazy" in Nashville, Tennessee
Kenyan political activistJomo Kenyattais released from jail after 9 years, having been imprisoned during the 1952 Mau Mau rebellion with other nationalist leaders by British authorities
Democratic Convention opens in Chicago and goes on to nominateHubert Humphrey
The Social and Democratic Labour Party (SDLP) is established withGerry Fittas the first leader of the party
Film adaptation of John Pielmeier's stage drama "Agnes of God," directed by Norman Jewison and starring Meg Tilly,Anne Bancroft, andJane Fonda, premieres
Ian Bothamtakes his world-record 356th Test cricket wicket against New Zealand at The Oval
"Dirty Dancing," a film directed by Emile Ardolino and starringPatrick Swayzeand Jennifer Grey, opens in the US
Conservative coup in the Soviet Union is crushed by popular resistance led byBoris Yeltsinin three days
Marvel superhero film "Blade," starringWesley Snipes, is released
P. W. Bothais found guilty of contempt for repeatedly ignoring subpoenas to testify before South Africa's Truth and Reconciliation Commission

American super-swimmerMichael Phelpswins his sixth gold medal of the Athens Olympics even though he doesn't swim in the final of the men's 4 x 100 m medley relay; the US wins in a world record time of 3:30.68
Striker Carli Lloyd scores in extra time as United States beats Brazil 1-0 to win the women'sfootball gold medalat Beijing Olympics

Kenyan runnerEliud Kipchogewins the Olympic men's marathon gold medal at the Rio de Janeiro Games in a time of 2:08:44; Feyisa Lilesa (Ethiopia) is second in 2:09:54, and Galen Rupp (US) is third in 2:10:05
Kevin Durantscores 30 as the US retains the Olympic men's basketball title with a comfortable 96-66 win over Serbia at the Rio de Janeiro Games
Australian Prime MinisterMalcolm Turnbullcalls for and wins a leadership vote 48-35 over Home Affairs Minister Peter Dutton
Michael Cohen,President Trump's personal lawyer, pleads guilty to charges, including making illegal payments at the direction of Trump to women with whom Trump had affairs
Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson is named the highest-paid actor for a second year in a row, earning $89.4 million
American golferScottie Schefflermakes birdies on four of the final five holes to become the 12th player in PGA Tour history to shoot a 59 in the second round of the Northern Trust at TPC Boston
Korean pop groupBTSreleases the single "Dynamite," the first video to be watched more than 100 million times in 24 hours on YouTube
"House of the Dragon", prequel to "Game of Thrones" premieres on HBO, starring Paddy Considine,Matt Smithand Emma D'Arcy
Thierry Henryis named the new manager of the France national under-21 team
Movie adaptation of Richard Osman's "The Thursday Murder Club" premieres in London, starringHelen Mirren,Pierce Brosnan, Celia Imrie, andBen Kingsley
American inventor William Seward Burroughs patents the adding machine
Warsaw Pact forces complete their invasion of Czechoslovakia by arresting Czech leaderAlexander Dubčekand forcing him to sign the Moscow Protocols
Voyager 2 begins a flyby of Neptune
Nigeria goes three years without a case of polio in a landmark step toward the eradication of the disease
Philip II Augustus, 1st great Capetian King of France (1179-1223), born in Gonesse, France (d. 1223)
Jorge de Lencastre, Duke of Coimbra, Portuguese illegitimate son of King John II and claimant to the throne, born in Abrantes, Portugal (d. 1550)
Shimazu Yoshihiro, Japanese samurai and warlord, born in Kagoshima, Japan (d. 1619)
Francois de Sales, French bishop of Geneva, writer and saint, born in Sainte-Mondane, France (d. 1622)

Johann Friedrich Treiber, German historian and composer, born in Osthausen, Arnstadt, Schwarzburg-Sondershausen (d. 1719)

Hubert Gautier, French engineer, wrote 1st book on bridge building, born in Nîmes, France (d. 1737)

Ethel Caterham, oldest British person ever and current verified oldest living person in the world (116 years), born in Shipton Bellinger, England

Toe Blake, Canadian Hockey HOF left wing (Stanley Cup 1935 Montreal Maroons; 1944, 46 Montreal Canadiens) and coach (Stanley Cup 1956-60, 1965-66, 68 Montreal Canadiens), born in Victoria Mines, Ontario (d. 1995)
